$parser_requirement: Implementations MUST use a libseccomp-compatible parser that tolerates $-prefixed annotation keys at every nesting depth (top-level and within syscalls[] entries), OR MUST strip $-prefixed keys recursively before loading. Docker ≥ 20.10 and runc ≥ 1.1 tolerate natively at both depths. Parsers MUST interpret the clone-arg filter (group `clone-flag-filter-x86_64-aarch64-riscv64`) using Docker seccomp JSON semantics: `value` is the mask and `valueTwo` defaults to 0, so `op: SCMP_CMP_MASKED_EQ` matches on `(flags & mask) == 0` (i.e., none of the CLONE_NEW* bits set → ALLOW; any set → deny per default action). Raw libseccomp without the Docker adapter layer would otherwise invert the comparison. The Docker seccomp-to-BPF translator and runc ≥ 1.1 apply this convention natively; bespoke loaders targeting libseccomp directly MUST mirror it.
description: Seccomp profile for SOA-Harness Harbor benchmark containers. Deny-by-default with a baseline allowlist suitable for compilers, test runners, and scoring binaries. The normative deny list from §9.7.1 is explicitly enforced; clone3 is allowed with layered defense per §9.7.3. See $deployment_notes.

$deployment_notes.architecture_caveat: clone()-arg filtering uses syscall-arg index 0 (flags). On x86_64/aarch64/riscv64 this is correct. s390x/ppc64le place stack at arg 0 and flags at arg 1 — the clone-arg filter in this profile would bind the wrong argument. With s390x/ppc64le now present in archMap, the baseline allowlist applies; only the clone-arg filter is skipped on those arches per the `includes.arches` gate on the clone-flag-filter entry. Those arches remain best-effort for namespace defense and MUST rely on the other §9.7 defenses (cap-drop=ALL, userns disabled at host, no-new-privileges).

compatible: any modern glibc or musl libc image
base_image_compatibility.notes: clone3 is allowed so that modern musl libc thread creation works. Namespace creation via clone3 is defended by §9.7 Host Kernel Prerequisite (user.max_user_namespaces=0 or equivalent) plus cap-drop=ALL, not by seccomp.

host_kernel_prerequisite.canonical_location: SOA-Harness Core Specification §9.7 — Host kernel prerequisite; check_order and rationale are normative there

$deployment_notes.userfaultfd_denial: Intentionally denied. SOA-Harness benchmark workloads (compiler, test runner, scoring) do not require userfaultfd. CRIU, QEMU post-copy live migration, and some async-memory runtimes would fail under this profile.
$deployment_notes.explicit_deny_rationale: The §9.7.1 named syscalls are unreachable via default-deny already (absent from allowlist). The explicit SCMP_ACT_ERRNO entries are defense-in-depth: they make the policy explicit, survive future allowlist edits, and produce consistent EPERM rather than relying on the default action.
$deployment_notes.optional_hardening_tier: Deployments wanting finer-grained clone3 flag filtering MAY add a SECCOMP_RET_USER_NOTIF supervisor (Linux ≥ 5.0, libseccomp ≥ 2.5) that reads clone_args out of the calling process's memory. OPTIONAL, not required for §9.7 conformance.

syscalls.$comment: Common syscalls needed by compilers, test runners, and scoring binaries. Derived from Docker default minus §9.7.1 deny list and hardening deny list below. clone3 is allowed here (§9.7.3); its namespace flags are defended by cap-drop + host sysctl.
syscalls.comment: Baseline allowlist. clone3 is intentionally present — namespace defense is layered (cap-drop=ALL for 6/7 CLONE_NEW* flags; host user.max_user_namespaces=0 sysctl for CLONE_NEWUSER).

syscalls.$comment: For legacy clone(), syscall-arg index 0 holds the flags bitmap on these arches. Mask blocks CLONE_NEW* flags at the seccomp layer. This filter does NOT apply to clone3() — clone3's flags live in a struct. clone3 is unconditionally allowed above; its defense is cap-drop + sysctl. See $parser_requirement for Docker vs raw-libseccomp interpretation of op: SCMP_CMP_MASKED_EQ.

args.comment: Deny CLONE_NEWNS | CLONE_NEWCGROUP | CLONE_NEWUTS | CLONE_NEWIPC | CLONE_NEWUSER | CLONE_NEWPID | CLONE_NEWNET = 0x7E020000 = 2114060288. Prevents namespace creation via legacy clone().

syscalls.$comment: On s390x/ppc64le, clone() stack is arg 0 and flags is arg 1 — the arg-index-0 filter would bind the wrong argument. Best-effort: allow clone() and rely on cap-drop=ALL + host sysctl + no-new-privileges.
§9.7.1 normative deny list. Redundant with default-deny (these names are absent from the allowlist) but kept as documentation + defense-in-depth.
syscalls.comment: §9.7.1 deny list plus kexec_file_load and umount. EPERM (1).

syscalls.$comment: Additional hardening. chroot is unneeded inside Harbor containers; pivot_root is a container-escape primitive; process_vm_{readv,writev} are cross-process memory access; new-mount-API syscalls bypass mount()/umount2().
syscalls.comment: Hardening denies. The new mount API (fsopen/fsmount/fsconfig/fspick/move_mount) is an alternate path to the mount primitives §9.7.1 blocks; deny all.

syscalls.$comment: Matches Docker default deny list for obsolete, rarely-needed, or privileged syscalls. userfaultfd and unshare intentionally denied — see $deployment_notes.
syscalls.comment: Matches Docker default profile's deny list. unshare is denied to block an alternate namespace-creation path; clone3 is the allowed path, constrained by cap-drop + sysctl.
